purpose: Identifies mutually contradictory macro assumptions embedded across equities, credit, rates, and gold to flag mispriced assets and regime change catalysts
components: Equity valuation regime (P/E, ERP assumptions)
Credit spread regime (IG/HY spread levels vs default expectations)
Rates regime (term premium decomposition, r* assumptions in forwards)
Gold residual analysis (model fair value vs actual price)
Cross-consistency check: what r*/ERP combination is required for all four to be simultaneously correct
current_assessment: Four markets tell contradictory stories. Equities price soft landing, credit prices perfection, rates price persistent inflation/fiscal risk, gold prices credibility loss. No single macro scenario reconciles all four. The 5y5y forward is consistent with equity valuations only under narrow and potentially unrealistic assumptions.
limitations: Different markets can rationally price different probability distributions
Framework identifies inconsistency but cannot determine which market reprices
Relies on non-GAAP adjustments being correctly estimated to assess equity mispricing
Static snapshot that may not capture dynamic rebalancing

purpose: Predicts the modal resolution pattern for extended monetary transmission lag episodes based on historical base rates
components: Historical episode identification (1966-69, 1994-95, 2004-07, 2018-19)
Resolution classification (discontinuous vs gradual)
Similarity scoring against current cycle
Exception identification (1994-95 preemptive tightening)
Current cycle novel feature assessment
current_assessment: 3-for-4 base rate for discontinuous catch-up. Current cycle most resembles 2004-07 and 1966-69 templates. The maturity wall (H2 2026-H1 2028) is the most likely channel. A 2027-2028 expectations un-anchoring window from the 1966-69 template is the most concerning tail scenario.
limitations: Sample of only four episodes limits statistical power
Current cycle has novel features (private credit scale, AI productivity, post-pandemic fiscal)
Cannot determine timing of discontinuity
1994-95 exception shows preemptive action can prevent discontinuity

purpose: Provides a real-time monitoring framework with 10 falsifiable thresholds that discriminate between competing macro analogues and signal regime transitions
components: 5Y5Y breakeven (threshold >2.7% sustained → 1966-69 inflation path)
HY OAS (threshold >550bp → 2007 credit path)
SOFR-IORB spread (threshold >15bp sustained → 2019 QT endgame)
Japanese institutional repatriation (threshold >30% of predicted → BoJ normalization)
EUR/USD (threshold <1.02 sustained → ECB independence loss)
Private credit NAV markdowns (threshold >5% broad-based → maturity wall activation)
Gold-real rate residual (threshold >$800/oz → financial repression pricing)
DXY-PPP deviation (threshold >+22% → dollar correction acceleration)
PIK toggle activation (threshold >25% → credit stress crystallization)
CLO AAA spreads (threshold >200-225bp → CLO formation stoppage)
current_assessment: No thresholds currently breached. 5Y5Y at ~2.4% (below 2.7%), HY OAS at ~350bp (below 550bp), PIK at 12-18% (below 25%), CLO AAA at 155-170bp (below 200-225bp). System is in pre-stress territory with leading indicators trending toward thresholds.
limitations: Thresholds are approximate, not precise triggers
Multiple thresholds breaching simultaneously could create nonlinear interactions not captured
Signposts may lag actual regime change
Some thresholds (e.g., private credit NAV) depend on self-reported data

purpose: Discriminates whether the current cycle will resolve through the credit channel (2007 analogue) or the inflation channel (1966-69 analogue) to guide positioning
components: Credit channel indicators: HY OAS >550bp, private credit NAV markdowns >5%
Inflation channel indicators: 5Y5Y breakeven >2.7% sustained
Current levels assessment
Historical analogue mapping
Resolution implications for asset allocation
current_assessment: Current levels (5Y5Y ~2.4%, HY OAS ~350bp) have neither channel activated. Framework is inherently unresolved until data moves toward one set of thresholds. Both channels remain plausible.
limitations: Assumes one channel dominates; both could activate simultaneously
Current levels are far from both thresholds
Analogue matching is imprecise with small samples
Does not account for novel resolution channels

purpose: Segments EM currencies by dominant factor loadings rather than traditional EM-as-monolith approach, enabling differentiated exposure management
components: Tier 0: Tech-proxy currencies (TWD, KRW) with SOX/NASDAQ loadings 0.55-0.65
Tier 1: High-credibility EM with Fed swap line access
Tier 2: Traditional EM with mixed fundamentals
Tier 3: Frontier/distressed with PBOC BSA as potential backstop
Swap line access overlay (Fed vs PBOC vs dual-access)
current_assessment: Tier 0 currencies trading as de facto tech sector proxies, not EM. KRW and TWD factor loadings on SOX at 0.55-0.65. Traditional EM carry compressed below systematic allocation threshold (0.3-0.4 carry-to-vol). PBOC BSA network ($560B+) creating parallel safety net.
limitations: Tier 0 membership (especially ILS, PLN) is uncertain
Factor loadings may shift as tech cycle evolves
PBOC BSA effectiveness untested in crisis
Framework is descriptive; predictive power for regime transitions is unproven

purpose: Decomposes monetary policy transmission through commodity markets into four partially-offsetting sub-channels to avoid aggregation errors
components: Demand channel: Fed tightening reduces global demand, lowering commodity prices
Dollar channel: Fed tightening strengthens USD, lowering USD commodity prices
Cost-curve channel: Stronger USD lowers EM producer breakevens by 8-15%, keeping marginal supply online
Inventory channel: Rate-sensitive storage economics affect supply timing
current_assessment: Channels are partially offsetting. Dollar and cost-curve channels dampen demand channel effects. Net transmission through commodities is weaker than any single channel suggests. Gas storage surplus is not evidence of broken transmission (associated gas insensitive to monetary policy).
limitations: Offsetting magnitudes are imprecise
OPEC+ fiscal breakeven reflexivity adds nonlinearity
Channel dominance varies by commodity
China credit impulse may dominate Fed effects for some commodities

purpose: Systematically assesses whether confidence levels assigned to claims are appropriate relative to the evidence base, using a Tetlock-inspired framework
components: Descriptive vs predictive claim classification
Historical base rate assessment for predictive claims
Conjunction fallacy detection for multiplicative probability claims
n<=2 precedent discount (1.5-2.5 points suggested)
Availability bias identification
current_assessment: Descriptive claims in the knowledge base are generally well-calibrated. Predictive claims are systematically overconfident by +1.5-2.5 points. Toolkit bandwidth (conjunction fallacy) and BoJ shock (availability bias) are exemplary failures. Non-GAAP masking and r* uncertainty are exemplary successes.
limitations: The specific 1.5-2.5 point discount is itself imprecise
Methodology better at identifying directional miscalibration than providing corrected estimates
Difficult to apply in real-time when new evidence arrives
Other agents do not consistently apply the discount
